meta_title: Roofing in Margate, FL | Premier Impact Windows
meta_description: Expert roofing in Margate, FL. Hurricane-rated CertainTeed & West Lake systems, 170mph wind-rated, full replacements & repairs. A+ BBB. Free quotes!
h1: Trusted Roofing in Margate, FL — Hurricane-Rated Systems That Protect What Matters Most
intro:
Margate homeowners know all too well that a reliable roof isn't a luxury — it's a necessity. Tucked into central Broward County and surrounded by growing communities like Coconut Creek, Coral Springs, and Tamarac, Margate sits in one of Florida's most active hurricane corridors. With a 170 mph wind zone designation and nine significant storm events recorded since 1990, the roofs over neighborhoods like Oriole Estates, Coral Gate, Margate Estates, and South Margate face relentless punishment year after year. From intense summer thunderstorms to the lingering impacts of historic hurricanes, your roof is your home's first and most critical line of defense.

Approximately half of all homes in Margate were built before 1994 — meaning thousands of properties across ZIP codes 33063, 33068, and 33093 are carrying roofing systems that predate modern Florida Building Code hurricane standards. These aging roofs, many approaching or exceeding the 25-year mark, simply weren't engineered to handle the pressure gradients and uplift forces that today's code requires. With average home values sitting around $310,000, protecting your investment with a properly rated, professionally installed roof system isn't just smart — it's financially essential. A failing or substandard roof can devastate your home's resale value, void your homeowner's insurance, or leave your family exposed during the next named storm.

At Premier Impact Windows & Roofing, we've helped more than 500 South Florida homeowners protect their properties with high-performance roofing solutions built for our unique climate. We understand Margate's specific building requirements, its storm exposure history, and the insurance landscape Broward County homeowners navigate every year. Whether you're replacing a 30-year-old shingle roof in South Margate or upgrading to a premium tile system in Margate Estates, our team brings the craftsmanship, code knowledge, and brand partnerships to deliver a roof that performs when it matters most.

section_why.h2: Why Margate Homeowners Choose Premier Impact Windows & Roofing for Their Roofing Needs
section_why.body:
Premier Impact Windows & Roofing has built its reputation as 'The Kings of Service' by doing one thing consistently — showing up for homeowners in South Florida communities like Margate with quality work, transparent communication, and installations that stand the test of time and storm season. With over 60 years of combined experience across our installation team, we don't just replace roofs; we engineer long-term protection systems tailored to the specific conditions of Broward County.

What truly separates us from the competition is our 100% in-house installation model. Unlike many roofing contractors who subcontract labor — sometimes to crews with no direct accountability to your project — every Premier Impact Windows & Roofing technician is a trained, background-checked member of our own team. This means consistent quality control from the first nail to the final inspection. Our installers know Margate's permit process through the City of Margate Building Division inside and out, ensuring your project moves through inspections without delays or costly re-work.

We've completed roofing projects throughout Margate's core neighborhoods, from aging ranch-style homes in Oriole Estates to larger family homes near the Margate Sports Complex and Calypso Cove Aquatic Facility. We understand that homes in the 33063 and 33068 ZIP codes often feature low-slope or flat sections alongside traditional pitched roof areas, which require careful attention to drainage and underlayment detailing. Our team accounts for these architectural nuances in every project scope.

We also hold an A+ BBB rating, are fully licensed and insured in the state of Florida, and offer flexible financing options starting at just $87 per month with $0 down available — making a critical roof replacement accessible even for homeowners working within a tight household budget. When you invest in a new roof with Premier Impact Windows & Roofing, you're not just buying materials. You're buying expertise, accountability, and the peace of mind that comes with knowing the work was done right the first time.

section_history.h2: Margate's Storm History Makes a Strong Roof Non-Negotiable
section_history.body:
Margate's storm history reads like a reminder of just how vulnerable Broward County homes can be when roofing systems aren't up to the task. Hurricane Andrew in 1992 remains one of the most destructive hurricanes in U.S. history, and while it made landfall further south in Miami-Dade, its outer bands and sheer wind field caused significant roof damage across Broward County communities including Margate. That storm fundamentally changed how Florida approached building codes — but homes built before 1994 never got retrofitted to meet those new standards, and many still carry their original roofing systems today.

Hurricane Wilma in 2005 hit Broward County particularly hard, sweeping across South Florida from the southwest and creating sustained winds and prolonged pressure loading that exposed weaknesses in shingle adhesion, flashing details, and underlayment systems throughout the region. Margate neighborhoods experienced widespread shingle loss and water intrusion events that led to costly interior damage. For homeowners in Coral Gate and South Margate, Wilma was a wake-up call about the hidden vulnerability of roofs that looked intact but lacked proper wind resistance ratings.

Most recently, Hurricane Irma in 2017 brought tropical storm and near-hurricane force winds to the Margate area, again revealing that older roofing systems — even those that had survived previous storms — were deteriorating in ways that only became apparent under sustained loading. With nine storm events affecting the area since 1990, Margate has seen more than enough evidence that investing in a properly rated, hurricane-engineered roofing system isn't a precaution — it's a proven necessity for protecting your home and family.

section_code.h2: Florida Building Code and Broward County Roofing Requirements Explained
section_code.body:
While Margate is not located within Florida's High-Velocity Hurricane Zone (HVHZ) — a designation primarily applied to Miami-Dade and Broward coastal areas — that does not mean roofing in Margate operates under relaxed standards. On the contrary, Margate's 170 mph wind zone designation means all roofing systems installed here must be tested and approved to meet rigorous Florida Building Code (FBC) requirements for wind uplift, impact resistance, and system integrity. Products used in Broward County must carry appropriate FBC approvals and be installed strictly per manufacturer specifications and code-approved methods.

The Florida Building Code mandates that all roof replacements in Broward County — including those in Margate's 33063, 33068, and 33093 ZIP codes — require a permit pulled through the City of Margate Building Division before work begins. This isn't a formality; it's a consumer protection mechanism. Permitted work triggers required inspections at key stages of the installation, verifying that underlayment, fastening patterns, drip edge installation, and final shingle or tile application all meet current code. Homeowners who allow unlicensed contractors to install roofing without permits risk failed inspections, required tear-offs, and insurance claim denials after storm damage.

Premier Impact Windows & Roofing handles the entire permitting process on your behalf. Our team is fully familiar with Margate's submittal requirements, inspection scheduling, and the documentation needed for both new installations and re-roofing projects. We also ensure that every roofing system we install qualifies for wind mitigation report documentation — a critical piece that connects directly to your homeowner's insurance premiums. Every product we install carries valid Florida Product Approvals (NOA or FL Number), giving you confidence that your roof meets the law and your insurance carrier's requirements.

section_products.h2: CertainTeed Landmark Series and West Lake Tile — Built for Margate's Climate
section_products.body:
Premier Impact Windows & Roofing installs two industry-leading roofing systems ideally suited for Margate's demanding climate and building code environment: CertainTeed architectural shingles and West Lake concrete and clay tile systems. Both product lines carry the hurricane-rated credentials required for Broward County installations and are backed by robust manufacturer warranties — giving Margate homeowners confidence that their new roof will perform season after season.

The CertainTeed Landmark Series is our most popular architectural shingle option for Margate homes, and for good reason. These dimensional shingles feature a dual-layer construction that delivers exceptional wind resistance and a Class A fire rating. The Landmark Series is Wind Resistance Warranty rated for winds up to 130 mph (with enhanced installation procedures achieving higher ratings), and the product line meets Florida Building Code requirements for Broward County wind zones. For homeowners in neighborhoods like Oriole Estates and Coral Gate looking for a cost-effective, high-performance replacement for an aging shingle roof, CertainTeed Landmark delivers superior durability with curb appeal that can meaningfully lift a home's value. CertainTeed's SureStart PLUS warranty program also provides coverage transferable to new homeowners — an asset when it comes time to sell.

For Margate homeowners seeking a premium, long-duration roofing system, West Lake concrete and clay tile products offer unmatched longevity and storm performance. Concrete and clay tile systems are inherently heavier and structurally durable, designed to withstand the mechanical loading of sustained hurricane-force winds when installed with the proper underlayment and fastening systems. West Lake tile products are manufactured to meet Florida's rigorous testing standards and are approved for use in Broward County's wind zone. Tile roofing also offers excellent thermal performance, helping to reduce attic heat gain and improve energy efficiency in Margate's year-round hot climate — a meaningful benefit for homeowners managing monthly utility costs.

section_cost.h2: What Does a New Roof Cost in Margate, FL — and Is It Worth It?
section_cost.body:
Roofing costs in Margate vary based on home size, roof pitch, material selection, and the complexity of the existing system, but most full roof replacements for a typical single-family home in ZIP codes 33063 or 33068 range from $8,000 to $20,000 or more depending on whether you're choosing architectural shingles or premium tile. Given Margate's average home value of approximately $310,000, a quality roof replacement typically represents between 3% and 7% of total home value — and the return on that investment is backed by both market data and insurance savings.

Financing a new roof shouldn't be a barrier. Premier Impact Windows & Roofing offers flexible financing options starting as low as $87 per month with $0 down available for qualified homeowners. We partner with lending programs designed specifically for home improvement, allowing you to protect your home now and pay over time at manageable monthly rates. For Margate's lower-middle income community, this means you don't have to wait until your roof is actively leaking to take action — you can get ahead of the problem before it becomes a crisis that damages ceilings, insulation, and interior finishes.

From a return on investment perspective, a new hurricane-rated roof in Margate pays dividends in multiple ways. First, it restores and can increase your home's resale value — buyers in Broward County actively look for newer roofs and will often discount offers substantially on homes with aging systems. Second, it positions your home for favorable insurance pricing through wind mitigation credits (discussed further below). Third, it eliminates the ongoing cost of repeated repairs on a failing system that will eventually need full replacement anyway. When you factor in all three financial levers, a new roof with Premier Impact Windows & Roofing is one of the highest-ROI home improvements available to Margate homeowners.

section_insurance.h2: Lower Your Insurance Premiums With a Hurricane-Rated Roof in Margate
section_insurance.body:
Homeowner's insurance in Broward County has become one of the most significant expenses for Florida property owners, and Margate residents are no exception. The good news is that a new hurricane-rated roof — particularly one installed with documented wind mitigation features — can generate meaningful premium reductions through Florida's wind mitigation credit program. Under Florida law, insurers are required to offer premium discounts for verified wind mitigation features, and the roof is the single biggest factor in those calculations.

A wind mitigation inspection report, completed by a licensed inspector after your new roof installation, documents key attributes including roof covering type, attachment method, deck attachment, and the presence of a secondary water resistance barrier (SWR). CertainTeed Landmark shingles and West Lake tile systems installed by Premier Impact Windows & Roofing are specifically chosen and installed to maximize the number of favorable credits on these reports. In Margate, homeowners who upgrade from older, non-rated roofing systems to compliant hurricane-rated systems routinely report annual insurance premium reductions of hundreds to over a thousand dollars per year depending on their insurer and policy structure.

Common insurance carriers serving Margate homeowners — including Citizens Property Insurance, Universal Property & Casualty, and Heritage Insurance — all recognize wind mitigation credits as part of their premium calculation frameworks. Premier Impact Windows & Roofing's team will help you understand what documentation you need following installation and how to present your wind mitigation report to your insurance agent for the maximum available discount. Over a 10-to-20-year roof lifespan, these cumulative insurance savings can amount to thousands of dollars — making the investment in a properly rated, professionally installed roof system even more financially compelling for Margate families.

faqs.q: How do I know if my Margate home needs a full roof replacement or just a repair?
faqs.a: Several signs indicate it's time for a full replacement rather than a repair: your roof is 20+ years old (which applies to roughly half of Margate homes built before 1994), you have widespread shingle granule loss, multiple areas of water intrusion, sagging deck sections, or previous repairs that haven't held. Premier Impact Windows & Roofing offers a free roof assessment where our experienced team inspects your roof thoroughly and gives you an honest recommendation — repair when possible, replacement when necessary.

faqs.q: How much does a roof replacement cost in Margate, and what financing is available?
faqs.a: Most full roof replacements for Margate single-family homes range from $8,000 to $20,000 depending on size, pitch, and material choice. CertainTeed architectural shingles typically fall at the lower end of the range, while West Lake tile systems represent a premium investment. Premier Impact Windows & Roofing offers financing starting at $87 per month with $0 down options available for qualified homeowners, making it possible to get a hurricane-rated roof installed without a large upfront cash outlay.

faqs.q: Does my Margate roofing project require a permit from the City of Margate Building Division?
faqs.a: Yes, all roof replacements in Margate require a permit through the City of Margate Building Division before installation begins. This applies to both shingle and tile systems. The permit process includes plan review and inspections at key stages of the installation. Premier Impact Windows & Roofing manages the entire permit process on your behalf — from application through final inspection sign-off — ensuring your roof is fully code-compliant and your warranty and insurance coverage are protected.

faqs.q: How long does a roof replacement take in Margate, FL?
faqs.a: Most residential roof replacements in Margate are completed within one to three days for standard-sized shingle systems, once all permits are approved and materials are on-site. Tile roofing projects may take slightly longer depending on roof complexity. Permit approval timelines through the City of Margate Building Division typically add a few business days to the overall project timeline. Premier Impact Windows & Roofing will give you a clear project schedule before work begins and keeps you informed at every stage.

faqs.q: Will a new roof actually lower my homeowner's insurance in Margate?
faqs.a: In most cases, yes — significantly. After your new hurricane-rated roof is installed, a licensed wind mitigation inspector documents your roof's protective features. This report is submitted to your insurance carrier, who is required by Florida law to apply wind mitigation credits to your premium. Margate homeowners upgrading from older, non-compliant roof systems to modern hurricane-rated installations frequently see annual premium reductions of several hundred to over one thousand dollars, depending on their insurer and the specific features of the installation.

cta_headline: Ready to Protect Your Margate Home With a Roof Built for Hurricane Season?
cta_body: Get your free, no-obligation roof inspection from Premier Impact Windows & Roofing — the Kings of Service — and discover how a hurricane-rated CertainTeed or West Lake roofing system can protect your home, lower your insurance, and give you real peace of mind before the next storm season arrives.
